name: Укиё-э гравюра — Гатс
moment: Guts stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal three-quarter view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 78% of frame height, plated forward in a heavy braced stance with both boots firmly on a rocky outcrop, torso twisted slightly toward the viewer, his black segmented armor and dark traveling cloak rendered as flat poster-ink layers with visible wood-grain texture running through every large color field as if printed from a carved woodblock (mokuhanga), a stern weathered warrior's face with both eyes open and a calm hardened expression, his mechanical left arm plated in dark iron with a simple hinged joint at the forearm resting downward at his side, his right hand wrapped tight around the grip of his massive iron greatsword, the blade resting diagonally across his shoulder and behind his back, its blunt slab-thick silhouette rendered in flat iron-grey with a single carved highlight edge like a printed woodblock key-line, short cropped dark hair. ANATOMY: exactly two arms (one flesh, one mechanical) and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure, no floating alternate pose anywhere in the composition. Behind him a monumental ukiyo-e wave modeled on Hokusai's Great Wave curls up and over the upper third of the canvas, its claw-like foam fingers rendered in the same flat woodblock-printed indigo-and-white layers, cresting just above his sword tip but never overlapping his face, small Hokusai-style cloud-band of pale grey mist sitting low behind his boots. Two small round vermilion hanko-style stamp cartouches (artist/publisher seal cartouches, blank decorative seal-marks with a simple geometric mon-crest, no invented kanji beyond the ones specified elsewhere on the print) sit in the upper corners of the canvas as traditional ukiyo-e print border ornaments.
art_style: японская гравюра укиё-э (мокухан, стиль школы Кацусика Хокусай/Утагава Куниёси): плоские заливки без градиентов, всюду читается фактура деревянного оттиска (грубое зерно доски, лёгкие непропечатанные разрывы по краям крупных плашек), контур фигуры — толстая продавленная чёрная линия как от резной доски, вода и облака построены классическими стилизованными завитками-когтями, лёгкое смещение цветных слоёв друг относительно друга (offset misregistration) имитирует ручную печать в несколько прогонов
typography: Main phrase 'HUNT OR BE HUNTED' is set inside a long horizontal woodblock-printed title cartouche (a rectangular ruled panel like the title cartouche on a classic ukiyo-e sheet) positioned along the lower third of the canvas beneath Guts's boots, cartouche fill flat aged-parchment cream with a thick carved black double-rule border (outer rule 0.03x cap-height, inner rule 0.015x cap-height, gap between rules 0.02x cap-height), the phrase itself set in a bold hand-carved brush-caps display face (uneven carved stroke edges, like hand-cut woodblock lettering, NOT a clean digital sans), fill solid sumi-ink black, cap-height 0.085x figure width, word gap 0.05x cap-height so all four words read clearly separated, each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column ガッツ (Guts, his name) is placed as a traditional red hanko-stamp cartouche in the lower-right margin beside his trailing leg, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat vermilion-red carved stamp-column matching classic ukiyo-e artist-seal style, glyph size 0.075x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: indigo blue, sumi-ink black, vermilion red, aged parchment cream, iron-grey armor, muted ochre wave-foam

name: Витраж — Кэн Канэки
moment: Kaneki Ken stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 76% of frame height, centered directly beneath the peak of a pointed gothic arch, standing in a still, upright half-turned stance, torso facing the viewer, head turned slightly to one side, his messy white hair catching light as flat luminous glass-segment highlights, his face split by the composition's central light/dark divide — one eye with a striking glowing red iris (his left eye, canon) rendered inside a small circular lead-lined lens-segment like a rose-window medallion, the other eye a calm human grey, wearing a fitted black long coat over a dark shirt, both hands loose at his sides in stillness, from his upper back four flowing crimson ribbons of energy extend outward and upward like radiating light-rays, each ribbon rendered as an elongated shard of glowing red glass-segment fanning symmetrically behind his shoulders, echoing the stained-glass ray motif rather than looking like a weapon in motion. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, the crimson ribbons are a separate back-appendage and do not replace or duplicate his limbs, no extra or duplicated arms/legs, no second figure anywhere in the composition. The whole scene sits inside a tall gothic stained-glass window frame filling the entire canvas: a lead-came (thick black outline) traces every color segment exactly like real leaded glass, the window's pointed arch top is echoed by a smaller tracery rose-medallion directly above his head, the left half of the background (behind his striking-eye side) rendered in deep glowing blues and blacks, the right half (behind his human-eye side) rendered in warm glowing ambers and soft creams, the two halves meeting on the strict vertical centerline behind him — a clear light/dark duality split down the center of the whole panel.
art_style: готический церковный витраж: толстые чёрные свинцовые контуры (came) вокруг каждого цветного сегмента, сами сегменты — плоские насыщенные полупрозрачные заливки будто подсвеченные изнутри, никаких мягких растушёвок, стрельчатая арка и розетка-медальон как архитектурное обрамление, дуальность света и тьмы читается по всей композиции
typography: Main phrase 'WHAT IS 1000 MINUS 7' is set inside a narrow horizontal leaded-glass banner-segment spanning the width of the arch directly below the small rose-medallion tracery above his head (a proper stained-glass banner strip, not a floating overlay), banner fill flat deep-cobalt glass with the same thick black lead-came border as the rest of the window (0.03x cap-height), the phrase set in a tall gothic blackletter display face (narrow, high-contrast, pointed serifs matching the arch's gothic architecture), fill luminous pale-gold glass color with a thin black lead outline around every letter consistent with the came style, cap-height 0.075x figure width (reduced to fit five words legibly across the narrow banner), word gap 0.045x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column カネキ (Kaneki, his family name) is set as a small leaded glass panel in the lower-left margin of the window frame beside his leg, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as bold flat crimson glass matching his glowing red eye with a thin black lead outline, glyph size 0.07x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: deep cobalt blue, emerald green glass segments, glowing crimson red, pale gold light, warm amber, black lead-came outline
chroma_note: ВНИМАНИЕ: витражная палитра содержит крупные изумрудно-зелёные сегменты стекла (emerald green glass segments), поэтому хромакей для сведения фона на этом принте — СИНИЙ (chroma: blue), а не зелёный.

name: Психоделика 70-х — Сатору Годжо
moment: Satoru Gojo stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 77% of frame height, blindfold removed and pushed up into his messy white hair, both of his brilliant glowing bright blue eyes wide open with soft luminous intensity, a faint knowing smirk on his face, standing in a loose confident stance with weight on one hip, one hand raised beside his head in a relaxed open-palm gesture, the other hand resting loosely in the pocket of his flowing dark high-collared jacket, the jacket and trousers rendered with liquid melting poster-groove distortion at the edges (fabric folds bending into soft swirling psychedelic ripples rather than straight cel-shaded creases) while his face and anatomy remain clearly readable and correctly proportioned. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure or duplicate pose anywhere in the composition. Around him, concentric liquid spirals in vibrating complementary colors radiate outward from behind his raised hand exactly like a 1970s concert poster's optical-vibration rings, the spiral bands warping slightly around his silhouette without ever obscuring his face or hands, a soft rainbow halo-glow rimming his whole figure where the spiral meets his outline.
art_style: психоделия концертных афиш 1970-х (в духе Wes Wilson/Victor Moscoso): плавящиеся жидкие контуры, вибрирующие дополнительные цвета на стыках плашек создающие оптическую вибрацию, спиральные концентрические узоры вместо фона, никакой чёткой прямой линии — всё слегка перетекает и закругляется
typography: Main phrase 'THE HONORED ONE' is rendered as a classic liquid-melting groove-poster lettering block (letterforms bulging, dripping and bending into each other's space like hand-lettered 1970s psychedelic type, each letter a different vibrating color from a tight rainbow-groove palette so the word itself pulses optically) arcing gently across the lower third of the canvas beneath Gojo's feet, following the innermost curve of the spiral background, cap-height 0.10x figure width, word gap 0.055x cap-height kept legible despite the liquid distortion so all three words read clearly, each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column ゴジョウ (Gojo, his family name) is placed in the upper-right margin beside his raised hand, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered in the same liquid-groove style but simplified for legibility (letterforms softly rounded, not overly distorted), fill glowing sky-blue matching his eyes, thin magenta halo-outline, glyph size 0.08x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly four glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.045x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: hot magenta, electric sky-blue, sunflower yellow, deep violet, tangerine orange, optic-white highlight

name: Панк-зин коллаж — Пауэр
moment: Power stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 76% of frame height, wild pink-blonde hair with two curved red devil-horns jutting from her scalp, a manic fang-baring grin stretched wide across her face, sharp cat-slit pupils in her eyes, standing in a loose off-balance lunging stance with one boot forward, wearing her Public Safety uniform jacket half-open over a dark undershirt, one hand raised holding a dripping blood-formed spiked mace/flail (her canon blood-fiend weapon, rendered as a solid coagulated dark-crimson clump studded with hardened blood spikes on a short chain), the other hand clenched in a clawed gesture at her hip, small spatters of blood flicking off the weapon's arc frozen mid-swing. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure anywhere in the composition. The entire background behind her is built as a torn-paper punk-zine collage: overlapping rectangular scraps of newsprint, magazine texture and grid-lined notebook paper at conflicting rotations, strips of visible masking tape crossing some of the paper-scrap seams, a fine xerox-photocopy grain and speckle noise laid over the whole print, small ink spatter and blood-fleck marks scattered across the paper scraps echoing her weapon.
art_style: панк-зин фотоксерокопия/коллаж: рваные края бумажных вырезок под разными углами, полосы скотча поверх стыков, зернистый ксероксный шум и лёгкие царапины по всей поверхности, высококонтрастный чёрно-белый подслой с яркими плашками поверх — вырвиглазная DIY-эстетика самиздата
typography: Main phrase 'BLOOD FIEND' is built as a classic ransom-note collage: every single letter is its own individually torn paper scrap cut from a different fake newsprint/magazine source (each letter a different font style, weight and slightly different off-white/cream paper tone, each scrap rotated a few degrees off the baseline and given its own tiny drop-shadow so it visibly sits pasted above the collage background), the two words arranged on a single slightly uneven line across the upper third of the canvas above Power's horns, cap-height 0.11x figure width, word gap 0.06x cap-height (a visibly wider torn-paper gap between the two ransom-note words), each word appears exactly once. Secondary line 'POWER!!' is set in a separate bold spray-stencil poster style (thick stencil-cut caps with rough spray-paint edge texture, exclamation marks included as part of the word), fill flat blood-crimson with a thin cream-white stencil-bridge outline, positioned as a stamped diagonal banner crossing the lower-right corner of the canvas near her weapon hand, cap-height 0.075x figure width, appears exactly once (this is a distinct secondary tag line, not a repetition of the main phrase). The vertical katakana column パワー (Power, her name) is placed in the lower-left margin beside her trailing leg, outside the figure's silhouette, cut as its own torn paper-scrap column matching the ransom-note technique, fill solid black ink on a cream scrap with a thin red edge, glyph size 0.08x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: newsprint cream, blood crimson, matte black ink, hot pink hair accent, masking-tape tan, xerox-grey noise

name: Ар-деко золото — Йор Форджер
moment: Yor Forger stands full figure, SINGLE FIGURE, one clear frontal view only (no second view, no model-sheet, no alternate angle anywhere on the print), the figure occupying 75% of frame height, centered and symmetrical in a poised, elegant stance, long straight black hair falling past her shoulders, calm red eyes with a composed faint smile, wearing her fitted black dress with a modest neckline, standing with one leg subtly forward for a graceful silhouette, both hands holding a pair of slim silver stiletto throwing-daggers crossed elegantly in front of her at waist height (blades pointed outward, a poised assassin's presentation rather than mid-action combat), a single dark red rose held delicately between two fingers of one hand near the crossed blades. ANATOMY: exactly two arms and two legs, correct human anatomy, no extra or duplicated limbs, no second figure anywhere in the composition. She is framed by a fully symmetrical art-deco border: tall geometric golden sunburst fan-rays radiate outward from directly behind her head and shoulders filling the whole upper background (Gatsby/Chrysler-Building style stepped geometric rays, evenly spaced, alternating bands of bright gold and deep bronze), the outermost edge of the canvas bordered by a thin elegant rectangular deco frame-line with stepped corner ornaments, the whole composition strictly mirror-symmetrical left to right around the vertical centerline through her body.
art_style: ар-деко (эстетика Гэтсби / Крайслер-билдинг): геометричные золотые веерные лучи, строгая симметричная рамка со ступенчатыми угловыми орнаментами, элегантные тонкие линии, плоские насыщенные заливки без грубого целл-шейдинга, ощущение роскоши и точной геометрии
typography: Main phrase 'THORN PRINCESS' is set inside a slim horizontal rectangular gold plaque positioned directly beneath Yor's crossed blades at the lower third of the canvas, plaque fill flat deep-bronze with a thin bright-gold stepped deco border (0.025x cap-height outer line plus a matching stepped corner motif at each of the four plaque corners), the phrase itself set in an elegant thin geometric deco capitals display face (tall, evenly weighted strokes, wide letter-tracking typical of 1920s deco titling), fill solid bright metallic gold, cap-height 0.08x figure width, word gap 0.09x cap-height (deco titling reads with generous open tracking between words), each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column ヨル (Yor, her given name) is placed inside the lower-left margin between her leg and the deco frame-line, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat gold stamp-style column matching the plaque's metallic tone with a thin bronze keyline, glyph size 0.075x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly two glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.045x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: bright metallic gold, deep bronze, matte black dress, blood-red rose accent, cream ivory background field
